perturbation.original_text: The HOF Entities shall consult with Constellation to develop a comprehensive energy strategy with respect to the Products & Services listing in Exhibit A, which reflects sustainable product solutions, competitive market pricing (incorporating completion of the Supplier Component Questionnaire in the form of Exhibit B hereof for the applicable HOF Entity or affiliate), periodic reporting and analytics, regulatory/market intelligence and infrastructure services and equipment.
perturbation.changed_text: The HOF Entities shall consult with Constellation to develop a comprehensive energy strategy with respect to the Products & Services listing in Exhibit A, which reflects sustainable product solutions, competitive market pricing (incorporating completion of the Vendor Component Questionnaire in the form of Exhibit B hereof for the applicable HOF Entity or affiliate), periodic reporting and analytics, regulatory/market intelligence and infrastructure services and equipment.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Supplier' is replaced with 'Vendor' in Section 1.1(a). This creates confusion because the original text refers to the document to fill as the 'Supplier Component Questionnaire' in Exhibit B, but this change implies the form is now referred to as a Vendor Component Questionnaire, creating ambiguity and contradiction, as Exhibit B remains unchanged and still refers to 'Supplier'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) The HOF Entities shall consult with Constellation to develop a comprehensive energy strategy with respect to the Products & Services listing in Exhibit A, which reflects sustainable product solutions, competitive market pricing (incorporating completion of the Vendor Component Questionnaire in the form of Exhibit B hereof for the applicable HOF Entity or affiliate), periodic reporting and analytics, regulatory/market intelligence and infrastructure services and equipment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Supplier' with 'Vendor' while Exhibit B, which is referenced in both the original and changed text, still refers to 'Supplier'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the contradiction arises because the document now uses both 'Supplier' and 'Vendor' seemingly interchangeably, creating confusion and potential unenforceability as it's unclear which entity is required to complete the form in Exhibit B. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All purchases of Products & Services from Constellation by the HOF Entities or their respective affiliates pursuant to this Agreement shall be made pursuant to a separate agreement executed between an HOF Entity or affiliate and Constellation which may include a master retail electricity supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-1, master retail natural gas supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-2, transaction confirmation in substantially the form of Exhibit C-3 or similar document or agreement (each, a "Second Level Agreement"). In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between this Agreement and any Second Level Agreement negotiated after execution of this Agreement and not substantially in the form attached hereto, this Agreement shall control except to the extent that the Second Level Agreement expressly refers to this Agreement and states that the specific provision thereof shall be deemed to amend and supersede a specifically identified provision hereof (in which case such specific provision of the Second Level Agreement shall control over such specifically identified provision hereof).
perturbation.changed_text: All acquisitions of Products & Services from Constellation by the HOF Entities or their respective affiliates pursuant to this Agreement shall be made pursuant to a separate agreement executed between an HOF Entity or affiliate and Constellation which may include a master retail electricity supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-1, master retail natural gas supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-2, transaction confirmation in substantially the form of Exhibit C-3 or similar document or agreement (each, a "Second Level Agreement"). In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between this Agreement and any Second Level Agreement negotiated after execution of this Agreement and not substantially in the form attached hereto, this Agreement shall control except to the extent that the Second Level Agreement expressly refers to this Agreement and states that the specific provision thereof shall be deemed to amend and supersede a specifically identified provision hereof (in which case such specific provision of the Second Level Agreement shall control over such specifically identified provision hereof).
perturbation.explanation: Changing "purchases" to "acquisitions" introduces an inconsistency. Throughout the document, including in the later clauses of the same section and other sections (e.g. 1.4 Warranty referencing 'all purchases'), the term "purchases" is consistently used, so by making this change in section 1.1(d) without clarifying if both words can be used as the same, creates confusion. This leads to the question of whether all the conditions and clauses with "purchases" will still apply for "acquisitions".
perturbation.contradicted_text: Constellation's standard warranty as stated in the applicable Second Level Agreement (the "Warranty") shall apply to all purchases of Products & Services made pursuant to this Agreement. Without limiting the Warranty, Constellation represents, warrants and covenants that all Products & Services delivered in connection with this Agreement or any Second Level Agreement will be and will have been produced and/or provided in compliance with all applicable laws. 2 Source: GPAQ ACQUISITION HOLDINGS, INC., S-4/A, 1/23/2020 1.5 Replacement Products & Services. In the event that Constellation shall fail to timely provide to the HOF Entities or any of their respective affiliates any Products & Services in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, the default and remedies provisions of the applicable Second Level Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.justification: The change from "purchases" to "acquisitions" creates a potential in-text contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly refers to "all purchases of Products & Services." By changing "purchases" to "acquisitions" in the first clause without further clarification, it introduces ambiguity as to whether the warranty and other provisions applicable to "purchases" also apply to "acquisitions." This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Since later sections refer to 'purchases,' the alteration introduces uncertainty about whether the conditions and clauses related to 'purchases' will apply to 'acquisitions,' thus contradicting the original intent and scope of the agreement and creating a potential legal issue.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the issuance of a certificate of substantial completion for a facility listed on Exhibit D is delayed by a period of 6 months or longer from the last day of the quarter indicated on Exhibit D for that facility, the HOF Entities will provide prompt written notice to Constellation of such delay, and the Sponsorship Fees will be reduced, to the extent applicable, as set forth in Exhibit H. Any construction delay shall not impact the HOF Entities' contractual commitment or timelines as it relates to the obligations set forth in this Agreement related to EME financing. Any commodity supply agreements with the HOF Entities related to the delayed facility shall be extended in term by the number of months of the certificate of substantial completion delay at then current market pricing, except to the extent that an existing agreement is in place with respect to such delayed facility or supply quantity purchased.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the issuance of a certificate of substantial completion for a facility listed on Exhibit D is delayed by a period of 6 months or longer from the last day of the quarter indicated on Exhibit D for that facility, the HOF Entities will provide prompt written notice to Constellation of such delay, and the Sponsorship Fees will be reduced, to the extent applicable, as set forth in Exhibit H. Any construction delay shall not impact the HOF Entities' contractual commitment or timelines as it relates to the obligations set forth in this Agreement related to EME financing. Any commodity supply agreements with the HOF Entities related to the delayed facility shall be extended in term by the number of months of the certificate of final completion delay at then current market pricing, except to the extent that an existing agreement is in place with respect to such delayed facility or supply quantity purchased.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "certificate of substantial completion" to "certificate of final completion" creates an inconsistency. The agreement consistently refers to 'certificate of substantial completion' until Section 1.6. Since the contract does not explicitly state these terms are identical, changing the wording creates a potential ambiguity regarding which specific certification triggers the adjustments to the commodity supply agreements. This ambiguity could affect the agreement’s enforcement, particularly since the standard for substantial completion can differ significantly from final completion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the issuance of a certificate of substantial completion for a facility listed on Exhibit D is delayed by a period of 6 months or longer from the last day of the quarter indicated on Exhibit D for that facility, the HOF Entities will provide prompt written notice to Constellation of such delay, and the Sponsorship Fees will be reduced, to the extent applicable, as set forth in Exhibit H. Any construction delay shall not impact the HOF Entities' contractual commitment or timelines as it relates to the obligations set forth in this Agreement related to EME financing. Any commodity supply agreements with the HOF Entities related to the delayed facility shall be extended in term by the number of months of the certificate of final completion delay at then current market pricing, except to the extent that an existing agreement is in place with respect to such delayed facility or supply quantity purchased.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'certificate of substantial completion' with 'certificate of final completion.' The original text consistently uses 'certificate of substantial completion.' Since the contract does not define these terms as equivalent, changing the wording introduces ambiguity. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text in this case, is now inconsistent with the rest of the document, creating a contradiction regarding which certification triggers the commodity supply agreement extension. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The distinction between substantial and final completion is meaningful and could lead to disputes.
